scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Demosthenes is an Athenian orator describing the specific laws governing Athenian citizenship and eligibility for the archonship.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The text describes the 'basanos', a specific Athenian legal procedure for the judicial torture of slaves for testimony.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Dated to the floruit of Demosthenes and the specific delivery of the speech Against Neaera in approximately -340.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The 'Old Oligarch' (Pseudo-Xenophon) is critiquing the democratic institutions of the Athenian state.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Modern scholarship generally dates this work to the early years of the Peloponnesian War, roughly -425.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The text discusses the right of speech (isegoria) as practiced in the Athenian assembly and council.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The author explicitly compares Athenian customs regarding slaves to those of other states, focusing on Athenian law.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The rule details the legal status and physical protection of metics (resident aliens) in Athens.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: This treatise describes the functioning of the contemporary Athenian democracy circa -425.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The passage describes the Athenian liturgical system (choregia, trierarchy) which were civic obligations in Athens.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The rule describes Athenian imperial policy toward the elite classes within its subject 'allied' cities.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Addresses the construction of public gymnasia and baths by the Athenian state for its citizens.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Describes Athens' use of its naval supremacy (thalassocracy) to control trade for its allies.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Discusses the strategic and economic vulnerabilities of different classes of Athenian citizens during war.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Describes the political and legal maneuvers used by the Athenian democratic body (the People) to manage external obligations.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Describes rules governing Old Comedy and the limits of public satire as practiced in Athens.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Describes the collective protection from libel enjoyed by the Athenian Demos in public performances.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Details the specific legal calendar and adjudication for liturgy disputes (antidosis and performance) in Athens.
